announcements.title: Welcome to the Department of Chemistry!
announcements.content: Doc Learning Hub is now online. Access your study guides, lecture notes, and complete your module quizzes natively inside the app.

announcements.title: General Chemistry Lab Safety
announcements.content: All students enrolled in General Chemistry must complete Module 1: Laboratory Safety & Practices before entering the chemistry labs.

modules.title: Module 1: Atomic Structure & Periodicity
modules.desc: Introduction to atomic history, quantum mechanical model, and periodic properties.

questions.question: Which quantum number determines the shape of an atomic orbital?
An electron moves to a higher energy level by emitting a photon.
What is the name of the principle stating that no two electrons in an atom can have the same set of four quantum numbers?

assignment.title: Orbital Configuration Sheet
assignment.desc: Draw the orbital diagram for Phosphorus (Z=15) and answer the written problems in the form.

modules.title: Module 1: Alkanes & Stereochemistry
modules.desc: Understanding molecular conformations, Newman projections, and chirality.

questions.question: Which conformer of n-butane is the most stable?
Enantiomers have identical physical properties in a symmetric environment.
What is the term for stereoisomers that are not mirror images of each other?

assignment.title: Chirality & R/S Configuration Exercises
assignment.desc: Identify R/S configuration for the molecule centers shown in the worksheet and submit your drawings.

modules.title: Module 1: Titrimetric Methods of Analysis
modules.desc: Core principles of acid-base, precipitation, and complexometric titrations.

questions.question: What indicator is commonly used for standard strong acid-strong base titrations?
The equivalence point of a weak acid titration with a strong base is always exactly at pH 7.
What is the term for a solution of known concentration used in volumetric analysis?

assignment.title: Titration Curve Calculation Lab
assignment.desc: Submit your calculated pH values during the titration of 50.0 mL of 0.100 M acetic acid with 0.100 M NaOH.

modules.title: Module 1: First Law of Thermodynamics
modules.desc: Heat, work, internal energy, expansion processes, and state functions.

questions.question: For a cyclic process, what is the net change in internal energy (dU)?
What type of thermodynamic process occurs at constant volume?

assignment.title: Enthalpy of Expansion Calculations
assignment.desc: Solve expansion work problems for reversible and irreversible ideal gas systems.

modules.title: Module 1: Chemical Safety Guidelines & MSDS
modules.desc: Safety protocols, hazard labeling (GHS), and handling chemical spills.

questions.question: What is the correct action if a chemical splashes into your eyes?
choices: Rinse immediately in the eye wash station for at least 15 minutes
Wait until you see a doctor

questions.question: It is acceptable to pour organic solvent wastes down the sink if diluted with plenty of running tap water.
What is the acronym for the sheet detailing hazard, protective, and emergency details of a chemical?

assignment.title: GHS Pictogram Identification Sheet
assignment.desc: Identify the hazard classifications for all symbols shown on the sheet and write brief safety handling notes.

modules.title: Module 1: Proteins & Amino Acids
modules.desc: Syllabus on structural levels of proteins, amino acid properties, and peptide bonds.

questions.question: Which of the following amino acids contains a sulfur atom?
All amino acids found in proteins have the L-configuration.
What is the bond that links amino acids together in a protein chain?

assignment.title: Protein Folding Worksheet
assignment.desc: Identify hydrophobic/hydrophilic interactions and secondary structure motifs.
